hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic complementarity at position 5 with SSTR2-specific ECL2 residues and introducing steric bulk at position 11 will improve selectivity margin by strengthening SSTR2 binding while reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by balancing strong on-target binding with reduced off-target interactions. Iteration 15 shows that while strong ddG is achievable, the structural quality and selectivity need refinement. The next step is to prioritize structural improvements and off-target disruption at non-pharmacophore positions.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved pocket contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) that contribute to off-target binding (especially SSTR3/SSTR5).
Structural failures suggest poor geometry in the binding interface. Tightening the clash threshold will enforce better packing and reduce structural failures.
